articles.title: FactChecking Trump’s Contentious ‘Meet the Press’ Interview
articles.summary: President Donald Trump walked out of a sit-down interview with Kristen Welker on NBC’s “Meet the Press.” That happened after he made, or repeated, a number of false and unsupported claims — some of which Welker pushed ba

articles.title: Did Trump offer crypto rewards to FIFA World Cup fans? Don't be fooled
articles.summary: Trump's endorsement of cryptocurrency, including his promise to make the U.S. the world's "crypto capital," likely lent credibility to the rumor.

articles.title: Was Trump booed during national anthem at Knicks' NBA Finals game?
articles.summary: Matching social media videos to the livestream showed the crowd booed as the president appeared on the jumbotron.

articles.title: What we know about claim Melania Trump did 'lesbian photo shoot' for French magazine
articles.summary: Old photos of the first lady posing nude with another woman recirculated online during Pride Month 2026.

articles.title: Did murderer named Ronald McDonald kill 12 children in 1892? Here's the truth
articles.summary: According to the story, a late-19th-century serial killer shared a name with the future fast-food mascot.

articles.title: Did Netanyahu once say, 'America is a golden calf and we will suck it dry'? There's no proof
articles.summary: The alleged quote from Israel's prime minister has repeatedly resurfaced online since 2015, but evidence remains elusive.

articles.title: Was Florida woman arrested for handing out tequila samples in Costco?
articles.summary: The story originated from social media posts by The Dude Humor Report, which labels its content as satirical.

articles.title: Did slaves who escaped to Canada invent ice hockey? Unpacking the real history
articles.summary: Black Canadians didn't "invent" hockey, but some of the playstyles commonplace today originated from Black pioneers in the sport.

articles.title: Is Coleman lazy river pool real and sold at Costco?
articles.summary: Social media users shared an image promoting a "200-foot lazy river pool system," complete with inner tubes, a filter pump and a chlorine dispenser.

articles.title: Trump dozed off in Oval Office. Did he wake himself up with a fart, as video allegedly shows?
articles.summary: Social media clips purportedly showed the president waking himself up with a fart after dozing off during an Oval Office event.

articles.title: Did residents of French village ask Hegseth to skip D-Day ceremony?
articles.summary: It's unclear why the defense secretary didn't attend the ceremony in Langrune-sur-Mer.

articles.title: Unfounded claim Melania Trump was an escort before meeting her husband recirculates online
articles.summary: Melania Trump has denied the rumor since it first appeared in 2016, even obtaining damages in the settlement of a lawsuit against the Daily Mail.

articles.title: Are US, Israeli militaries integrating? Here's what US defense bill says
articles.summary: Social media users falsely claimed the two militaries would fully merge. While the bill outlines closer collaboration, many details remain unclear.

articles.title: Step away from claim 'House Bill 417' banned line dancing in several US states
articles.summary: The claim is part of a trend of social media posts, often produced using AI tools, sharing fake legislation.

articles.title: Did a German hotel send 'no Jews allowed' message to Israeli guest?
articles.summary: The hotel said the antisemitic response was sent after it mistook the Israeli booking inquiry for a fake request.

articles.title: Chick-fil-A didn't post statement about refusing to celebrate Pride month
articles.summary: The fast food chain has had a checkered history with regard to LGBTQ+ rights.

articles.title: Mark Twain falsely credited with quote about reading 'some obituaries with great pleasure'
articles.summary: Social media memes and items for purchase, including magnets, stickers and yard signs, have misattributed the quote to the American author.

articles.title: 20 claims we've fact-checked about Bill Clinton
articles.summary: From AI-generated criticisms of Trump officials to alleged hush money, here are some of our most popular investigations regarding the ex-president.

articles.title: Ohio Senate Candidates Spar Over Donations Tied, Loosely or Not, to Epstein

articles.title: Did Mark Zuckerberg's data center 'dry up' Rio Grande? Here's what records show
articles.summary: The rumor touched on concerns about tech companies' environmental impact.

articles.title: A Timeline of RFK Jr.’s Mixed Messaging on the Measles Vaccine

articles.title: Trump Distorts Maryland’s Primary Ballot Mix-up to Attack Mail-in Voting
